steps.title: Compare warming findings
steps.content: Text 1 cites non-boreal research showing that warming benefits invasive species more than native species. Text 2 describes an Alaskan study where higher summer temperatures did not produce significant growth variation in either the native or invasive species.

steps.title: Limit the challenge
steps.content: Choice A says Text 2 gives an alternative explanation, and Choice B frames the issue as methodology; neither happens. Choice D is too strong because Text 2 does not invalidate the non-boreal findings themselves. It questions whether those findings apply generally to Alaska's boreal context. The answer is C.
